overview: Jeskai Elementals operates as a tempo strategy that leverages disruptive sorceries and conditional ramp to outpace opponents. The deck uses Deceit to steal key spells or threats while generating card advantage, supported by Wistfulness to accelerate lands and draw cards when Elementals are on the battlefield. Roaming Throne provides flexible pump and counter manipulation to quickly scale small Elementals into formidable blockers or finishers.
win_conditions: Beat down with pumped Elementals to close out games
Generate overwhelming card advantage through Wistfulness and Deceit
Control board state by copying opponent removal or threats via Deceit

keep_criteria: Hands with at least one early Elemental or Roaming Throne
Hands containing Deceit paired with a land drop
Hands with Wistfulness and a playable creature
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing duals/fixing), a turn-1 or turn-2 play (1-drop Elemental or early interaction like Deceit), and at least one way to protect your board or counter threats.
Keep Roaming Throne only with 3+ cheap instants/sorceries to copy.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking interaction or early board presence
Hands with only high-cost spells and no ramp
Hands missing lands or card draw engines
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, or 2+ lands with only late-game threats and no interaction.

early_game: Play small Elementals to trigger Wistfulness's draw condition
Use Wistfulness to fetch lands and maintain mana advantage
Hold Deceit for opponent's turn to disrupt early threats
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a 1-drop Elemental or hold up Deceit/counter.
Turn 2: Develop board with another Elemental or cast interaction.
Sequence lands to ensure blue/red availability for counterspells/removal while maintaining white sources for protection.
If opening with Roaming Throne, play it on turn 2 or 3 once you have a stack of cheap spells to copy.
mid_game: Activate Roaming Throne to move counters onto key Elementals
Cast Deceit to copy opponent's removal or pump spells
Maintain board presence while cycling through card advantage
Hoard interaction until key threats or combo pieces are on the stack.
Use Wistfulness's death trigger to refill your hand when trading Elementals favorably.
Avoid overcommitting to the board if you lack answers to their sweepers or spot removal.
Manage your graveyard only if the format features relevant reanimation or milling threats.
Commit threats when you have interaction to protect them or can trade favorably.
Hold back big finishers if the opponent has sweepers or targeted removal.
late_game: Scale Elementals into unblockable threats using Roaming Throne
Leverage Deceit to replicate late-game finishers or sweepers
Close games with mana advantage and superior board state
Large, resilient Elementals combined with Roaming Throne copying removal/counters to clear the path.
Wistfulness can provide card advantage to outlast them.
Once you have board control and sufficient interaction, commit to dealing damage with evasive threats or copying removal to eliminate blockers.
Go all-in when you have board control, sufficient interaction to handle their answers, and a clear path to damage.
Commit when Roaming Throne is on board with multiple cheap spells to copy, or when the opponent has no sweepers or targeted removal in hand.

key_cards.name: Roaming Throne
key_cards.notes: Enter with a +1/+1 counter, then tap to move it to an Elemental. Use it to quickly scale a 1/1 into a 2/2 or larger, or to save a key creature from removal by boosting its toughness.

key_cards.name: Wistfulness
key_cards.notes: Play it when you control an Elemental to draw a card while putting a land onto the battlefield. Use it to accelerate into Deceit or maintain tempo against aggressive decks.

key_cards.name: Deceit
key_cards.notes: Cast during your opponent's turn to reveal their hand and copy a nonland card. Target removal to protect your board, or copy threats to swing the game. Always consider holding it up for surprise timing.

sequencing_tips: Hold Deceit until your opponent's turn to maximize disruption and avoid wasting it on a dead hand
Cast Wistfulness immediately after playing an Elemental to secure the card draw and land drop in one turn
Move counters from Roaming Throne only when an Elemental needs immediate size to block or attack
Prioritize copying opponent's removal over threats when your board is vulnerable
Common mistake: Overcommitting to the board without protection or tracking opponent's interaction.
Unique interaction: Roaming Throne can copy your own counterspells to protect multiple threats, or copy removal to clear multiple blockers.
Wistfulness dies to sacrifice outlets or combat, so plan trades carefully.
Watch for format-specific hate cards like graveyard hate or mass removal.
Always track opponent's hand size and available interaction to time your plays effectively.

matchups.advice: Use Deceit to copy early removal or pump spells, and prioritize blocking with pumped Elementals. Wistfulness helps you outpace their mana curve.
Play around their board wipes by holding Deceit to copy their counterspells or removal. Use Roaming Throne to quickly recover board presence after a sweep.
Disrupt their key pieces with Deceit during their turn. Use Wistfulness to accelerate into interaction before they assemble their win condition.
Race them with pumped Elementals while using Deceit to steal their value generators. Maintain tempo by trading efficiently and leveraging Wistfulness for card advantage.

Use cheap, multi-target, or uncounterable threats. Cast spells in response to their counterspells. Use Roaming Throne to copy your own counterspells or removal. Don't overcommit expensive spells to the stack; trade efficiently and wait for them to use interaction on lesser threats.

Apply early pressure to force errors. Use counterspells and removal to disrupt their mana development. Bring in more interaction post-sideboard. Prioritize killing their ramp sources before they can deploy finishers.

Win the resource war. Use better sequencing, sideboard cards, and card advantage. Prioritize keeping interaction to counter their key spells. Win by out-drawing and out-resolving. Track their hand size and available interaction to time your plays.

common_threats: Wide creature spreads that overwhelm early board presence
Board wipes that clear pumped Elementals
Counterspells that protect key combo pieces or threats
Direct damage spells that bypass blockers
Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Cyclonic Rift), targeted removal (Path, Swords to Plowshares), and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents save interaction for your key spells.
Track graveyard hate if they start milling or reanimating.
Pay attention to mana bases to predict available answers.
